question: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire before GTA VI?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if  if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution for a ceasefire will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Drake releasing his long-teased Iceman album before GTA VI's confirmed November 19, 2026 launch, with an 86% implied probability reflecting skin-in-the-game bets amid the project's year-long rollout. Recent catalysts include Drake's March 31 speech affirming the album drops "soon," DJ Akademiks' insider release window hints, and Adin Ross teasing snippets, fueling speculation of an imminent spring deployment despite past delays. GTA VI remains stable post its November 2025 delay announcement, with no verified shifts from Rockstar Games. Key watchpoints: Drake's next social media update or surprise drop, versus potential Iceman postponement; market closes July 31, 2026, splitting 50/50 if neither event occurs by then, heightening urgency for traders.

question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certain odds of 98.2% for "No" on GTA VI releasing before June 2026, driven by Rockstar Games' official announcement setting the launch for November 19, 2026—following two prior delays from the initial Fall 2025 window, first to May 26, 2026, and then six months later amid polishing needs for the ambitious open-world title. Take-Two Interactive reaffirmed this date in its February 2026 earnings call, with launch marketing slated to begin this summer, underscoring confidence in the timeline after massive development investment. Rockstar's history of prioritizing quality over deadlines, as with GTA V's long gestation, bolsters this positioning. Realistic upset scenarios remain slim: only a surprise early-access drop or certification breakthrough could shift it, but no verified reports suggest acceleration, leaving traders heavily positioned against a pre-June launch.

question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Harvey Weinstein's ultimate prison time remains fragmented, with no prison time edging out at 27.4% amid his ongoing #MeToo-era legal battles, closely trailed by 20-30 years (21.3%) and 10-20 years (19.5%). Driving this uncertainty is the March 4 announcement setting his third New York rape retrial for April 14 in Manhattan, following a 2025 split verdict—conviction on a criminal sexual act (up to 25 years possible upon sentencing) but deadlock on rape—coupled with a rejected new trial bid in January. His separate 16-year California rape conviction appeal lingers unresolved, while recent prison interviews highlight severe health decline (cancer, diabetes), fueling bets on potential pleas, compassionate release, or time served at age 74. Momentum hinges on retrial testimony and guild-like judicial precedents in high-profile overturns.

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche lead trader consensus at 19.9% implied probability to win the 2026 Stanley Cup, fueled by their dominant Western Conference record of 49-14-10 and Nathan MacKinnon's scoring tear, positioning them for the Presidents' Trophy amid a late-season surge. Tampa Bay Lightning (14.2%) and Carolina Hurricanes (11.0%) trail closely, bolstered by trade deadline acquisitions enhancing depth and championship pedigree, with Tampa tied atop the East at 98 points alongside surging Buffalo Sabres (5.3%). Montreal Canadiens (5.3%) and Dallas Stars (7.8%) reflect breakout momentum and wild-card contention, keeping the race tight as playoffs loom April 18—historical upsets, goaltending volatility, and bracket matchups underscore the crowded path for all top contenders.

question: Will the Boston Bruins win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Boston Bruins win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 37.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions, bolstered by their league-best 60-16 record and clinched No. 1 overall seed with home-court advantage throughout the playoffs after winning the 2025 Finals. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 16.8% following a nine-game win streak to reach 57-18, second-best mark, driven by Victor Wembanyama's dominance and elite defense. Boston Celtics at 11.1% benefit from recent East surges amid power rankings climbs, while Denver Nuggets (8.5%) hold steady despite tougher West path. Detroit Pistons top Eastern standings at 55-21 but trail due to conference disparity; key injury reports and final-week matchups could shift seeding before playoffs begin late April.
